Over the years, it has hosted two individual time trials. On stage 15 in 1979, it served as the starting point for a mountain time trial to Avoriaz, which ended in victory and the yellow jersey for Bernard Hinault, who dethroned Joop Zoetemelk on his way to his second overall Tour victory.Much earlier, in 1935, Évian was the finish of stage 5b, a 58-kilometre time trial starting from Geneva and won by Italy’s Raffaele di Paco."],"stageType":"PAS","locale":"en","_id":"5ae2ca61db944c43a4204edda1af03a0b3e50aa5a1d1d92e9f88db4d7c6f014a","_bind":"publication_en-2026-16","_origin":"news-2026-16","_updatedAt":1784632033193,"_parent":"stage-2026:873524f689a83bf204502034a2093e0dc0700615dad69b457b6971f23a478929","_key":"_id","_virtual":"news:two-time-trials-in-evian","model":"news"},{"locale":"en","stageType":"PAS","text":["Second at Plateau de Solaison, Tadej Pogacar now leads the mountains classification with 67 points. Or just TT bike all the way ?","event":{"name":"Le Tour de France","trigram":"tdf","bubble":"cycling","host":"https://www.letour.fr/"},"response":{"updatedBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com","text":"Hello ! Given the unique profile of this individual time trial, many teams certainly had to consider this crucial question leading up to the Tour de France. However, the vast majority of riders, and likely all the general classification favorites, are expected to ride with a time trial bike for the entire stage. This is because the Côte de Larringes (9.7 km at 4.3 %), despite its length, is a fast and steady climb where the top riders will maintain speeds of around 30 km/h. At that speed, an aerodynamic position is essential. Furthermore, with a seven-kilometre descent followed by nine flat kilometres to the finish, a time trial bike is indispensable for those sections. Finally, swapping bikes at the top of the Côte de Larringes would result in a costly loss of time, potentially around ten seconds, depending on the speed of the changeover.","createdAt":"2026-07-21T11:23:58+00:00","updatedAt":null,"publicationAt":"2026-07-21T11:23:00+00:00","createdBy":"mathieu.costagutta@gmail.com"},"updatedBy":null,"_id":"6a5f3f6264a56478f50436c4"},"_id":"question-6a5f3f6264a56478f50436c4","_bind":"publication_en-2026-16","_origin":"interactiveContent-2026-16","_updatedAt":1784633038661,"_parent":"stage-2026:873524f689a83bf204502034a2093e0dc0700615dad69b457b6971f23a478929","_key":"_id","_virtual":"interactiveContent:enundefined-undefined","model":"interactive","isClub":false},{"id":20788,"stage":{"timezone":"Europe/Paris","type":"PAS","length":26.1,"showGroups":false,"date":"2026-07-21T00:00:00+02:00","stage":16,"isCancelled":false,"lengthDisplay":26.1,"podiumDisplayMode":"instantpodium"},"socialContentLangs":[{"title":"Les coureurs à ne pas manquer","updatedAt":"2026-07-21T12:35:11+02:00","createdBy":"ganz","locale":"fr","createdAt":"2026-07-21T12:35:11+02:00","updatedBy":"ganz","id":20787},{"locale":"en","createdBy":"ganz","updatedAt":"2026-07-21T12:35:11+02:00","title":"Riders to follow","updatedBy":"ganz","id":20788,"createdAt":"2026-07-21T12:35:11+02:00"}],"embedCode":"

This is because the Côte de Larringes (9.7 km at 4.3 %), despite its length, is a fast and steady climb where the top riders will maintain speeds of around 30 km/h. This is because the Côte de Larringes (9.7 km at 4.3 %), despite its length, is a fast and steady climb where the top riders will maintain speeds of around 30 km/h.
